content: What did the Lord promise to give His people in place of a heart of stone?
explanation: Ezekiel 36:26 - The Lord removes the heart of stone and gives a tender heart. Fulfilled through the Holy Spirit.
options: A heart of flesh (a new heart)

content: What did the Lord say He would do when the shepherds failed to fulfill their duty?
explanation: Ezekiel 34:11 - The Lord is the true Good Shepherd. Jesus called Himself the Good Shepherd (John 10:11).
options: Let the flock fend for itself
He Himself will search for and care for the flock

content: What principle does Ezekiel 18:4 teach about responsibility for sin?
explanation: Each person is responsible for his own sin. No one is punished for another's sin.
options: The father bears the son's sin
The son bears the father's sin
The soul that sins shall die (each person is responsible for his own sin)

content: Does the Lord take pleasure in the death of the wicked?
explanation: Ezekiel 33:11 - I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked, but desire that the wicked repent and live.
options: No, the Lord wants the wicked to repent and live

content: What does Ezekiel's first vision describe?
explanation: Ezekiel 1:4-10 - A complex vision of the Lord's throne with 4 living creatures having the faces of a man, lion, ox, and eagle.
options: Four winged living creatures and wheels, the glory of the Lord

content: What is the meaning of the vision of the river flowing from the temple?
explanation: Ezekiel 47:9 - Wherever this water flows, there is life. Revelation 22:1 also describes a river of the water of life.
options: Living water flowing from the Lord's presence brings healing everywhere

content: What did the Lord promise to put within His people so they would obey?
explanation: Ezekiel 36:27 - The Lord will put His Spirit within them so they obey from within. Fulfilled at Pentecost.
options: My Spirit (the Holy Spirit) within you

content: The vision of the dry bones also prophesied what for the people of Israel?
explanation: Ezekiel 37:22 - The Lord promised to make them one nation under one king. Israel was restored as a nation in 1948.
options: The restoration and unification of Israel

content: The Lord said: "I sought for a man among them who would stand in the gap before Me on behalf of the land, but ___"
explanation: Ezekiel 22:30 - The Lord sought an intercessor but found none. The need for intercessors praying for the nation.

content: The Lord said to Ezekiel: "Do not ___ them and do not be afraid of their words"
explanation: Ezekiel 2:6 - The Lord urges courage to speak the truth even when opposed.

content: The Lord promised to set "My servant David" as shepherd. Whom does this prophesy?
explanation: Ezekiel 34:23-24 - The Shepherd David is Jesus Christ, descendant of David and eternal King (John 10:11).
options: Jesus Christ - the Good Shepherd from the lineage of David

content: Ezekiel 37 describes a vision of what valley?
explanation: Ezekiel 37:1-10 - The dry bones are restored into an army. A prophecy of the restoration of the people of Israel and the Lord's resurrection power.
options: The valley of dry bones brought back to life
